{"source": "camel-finance-members", "topic": "camel-indicatorquestions", "space": "indicatorquestions", "post_id": 91, "title": "Can we get strategy performance Backtests/Screenshots of the indicator.", "author": "", "date": "2025-02-22 07:10:53", "content": "I would like to request the inclusion of strategy performance backtests and accompanying screenshots for the current indicator. The goal is to obtain quantitative evidence that supports the indicator's viability as a trading strategy. Presently, the indicator is provided with minimal insight into the underlying quantitative tools, which leaves us without actual proof of its performance efficacy. A detailed backtest report would not only enhance transparency but also validate the indicator’s theoretical claims by demonstrating how it has performed historically across various market condition, particularly in a bear market. Implementing backtests and providing performance screenshots for the indicator will significantly enhance its credibility and practical utility. The primary objective is to supply quantitative proof that the indicator is not just a theoretical tool but a viable strategy backed by historical performance data. These backtests will help users: Validate the indicator's effectiveness by presenting key performance metrics such as win rate, drawdown, and profit factor. Gain deeper insights into how the indicator reacts under various market conditions. Optimize the indicator's parameters for better risk management and improved performance. Ensure transparency in the underlying quantitative methods, thereby building trust in the tool. Can you let us know if there are any backtests available? And if not, Why this won't be provided? (Its essentially a \"proof of work\") Also, I assume the indicator will work better on some assets, it would be good to know which asset is best aligned for the indicator to use it appropriately and the backtests will help identify these assets.", "comments_count": 4, "reactions_count": 2, "ingested_at": "2026-03-02T18:01:45.903889+00:00"}

(gold and bitcoin)", "author": "", "date": "2025-02-15 19:09:17", "content": "Hi Camel and crew! I posted this on Camels latest video but having just joined here thought this might be a better place for this discussion! I'm someone who is slowly getting my head around the cycles, is oriented towards using the next 5 -10 years to build a future for myself, and not really interested in short term trading. I've been spending the last few years clearing debts and have solidified a well paying career, and now ready to start building bitcoin and gold positions. Would it be a valid strategy to use the cycle lows to guide a DCA approach in a bit more of an intelligent way? I'm thinking of DCAing in to the gold daily cycle lows and trying to catch as much of the rest of this 8 year cycle as possible, while saving up some cash on the side to catch somewhere around the next BTC 4 year low and then beginning to DCA the cycle lows from there. Does this seem reasonable? Or am I confusing two approaches here that are best left separate? (i.e. cycle trading and DCAing)", "comments_count": 5, "reactions_count": 2, "ingested_at": "2026-03-02T18:01:45.904190+00:00"}

{"source": "camel-finance-members", "topic": "camel-resourceroom", "space": "resourceroom", "post_id": 492, "title": "Smart Journal", "author": "", "date": "2025-07-18 08:30:00", "content": "Hey everyone! I'd like to share a trading journal with you that I built a few years ago for the Pro Trader Lounge . I just found it again and can’t remember all the details (some parts might not have been 100% finished if I recall correctly), but it still includes some great features. It was the very first thing I ever built when I started coding – and honestly, it gave me massive headaches back then. But I was really proud of what I had created – kind of like that one bastard son who's always causing trouble but you still love him anyway 😄. Hope you enjoy it! Maybe someone wants to build on it and turn it into the next Camel Crew Smart Journal – I’d really appreciate that! ❤️🐫 About the Journal: !!! TEST IT FIRST BEFORE YOU RELY ON IT !!! It was originally made for CRYPTO TRADING , and again – THIS WAS MY FIRST PROJECT EVER , so PLEASE DON’T RELY ON IT BLINDLY! The idea was to COLLECT AS MUCH INFORMATION AS POSSIBLE WITH AS FEW INPUTS AS POSSIBLE . Features: You can install the Cryptosheets add-on for Excel and create a free account to get REAL-TIME CRYPTO DATA inside Excel. There’s a calculator where you can input your entry, stop loss (SL), and take profits (TPs), and it will provide you with DETAILED TRADE INFORMATION . Next is the Setups page , where you can enter your favorite setups to get STATISTICS about them. Once you've saved a setup in the sheet, you’ll only need to reference its setup number on the next page. Then there's the Main Page called “Trades” , with a GHETTO BLASTER-STYLE STATISTICS PANEL showing all your trade info. EVERYTHING WRITTEN IN WHITE FONT IS CALCULATED AUTOMATICALLY – ONLY FILL IN THE FIELDS WITH COLORED TITLES! On the right side of the tables, there are hidden formulas. YOU’LL NEED TO UNHIDE THEM TO CHECK OR MODIFY THEM. IF YOU ENTER RANDOM VALUES IN THESE CELLS, THE SHEET MAY BREAK. There’s also a Fees page where your maker and taker fees are calculated. Finally, there's the Crypto Prices page where you can add your favorite altcoins to track their prices (especially useful if you’re using the Cryptosheets add-on) and include them on the Trades page. Final Disclaimer: If I remember correctly, the sheet DOESN’T TRACK FUNDING FEES , so you’ll need to either: ADD THEM MANUALLY , WRITE A FUNCTION FOR IT , or USE THE NEW AI INTEGRATION IN EXCEL TO AUTOMATE IT . FEEL FREE TO MODIFY IT HOWEVER YOU LIKE – CHANGE IT, SELL IT, IMPROVE IT, WHATEVER. I might revisit and work on it again someday, but I’m not sure when I’ll have the time. Hope you enjoy it! Best, Alain rPTL Smart Journal – Kopie.xlsx", "comments_count": 4, "reactions_count": 5, "ingested_at": "2026-03-02T18:01:45.904591+00:00"}

synthesis_content:
# Daily Synthesis - 2026-02-21 (Cycle 2 - Evening Update)

Generated by: zeke-reason.sh (Claude Code autonomous reasoning layer)
Feed entries analyzed: 193 total, 30 scored across 2 evaluation cycles
KG state: 75 entities, 59 relationships, 9 associations, 8 domains

---

## System Status: DEGRADED
ALL 8 overnight queue jobs failed with gateway timeouts this morning (12:56-14:17 UTC).
Gateway PID 95262 is running, Spark reachable at 10.0.0.143:11434. All quality entries
today came from the afternoon Reason cycle (Claude Opus), not the overnight Spark queue.

---

## 3 Key Strategic Insights

### 1. TLT PRIORITY: Position Breakeven Analysis at 4.85% 20-Year Yield

The 20-year yield at 4.85% (DGS20, 2/19/2026) puts TLT approximately at $87-89.
Using TLT effective duration of ~17 years:

| Position | Strike | Qty  | Approx Breakeven TLT | Required Yield Drop | Probability |
|----------|--------|------|----------------------|---------------------|-------------|
| $90C     | $90    | 650  | ~$93-95 at expiry    | ~55-85bps to 4.0%   | Moderate    |
| $95C     | $95    | 300  | ~$98-100 at expiry   | ~85-105bps to 3.8%  | Low-Mod     |
| $101C    | $101   | 3150 | ~$104-107 at expiry  | ~115bps to 3.7%     | Low         |
| Shares   | -      | 1868 | At cost basis        | Any decline          | Moderate    |

Duration math: 17yr × 0.85% yield drop = ~14.5% price increase → TLT $87 × 1.145 = ~$100.
So a move from 4.85% to 4.0% gets TLT to ~$100 — making $90C and $95C profitable, but
$101C marginal. The $101C (3150 contracts) needs a recession-level catalyst.

Macro signals this week:
- Fed minutes: hawkish pause confirmed (bearish near-term for bonds)
- Jan jobs: 130k (below 150k trend — mildly supportive)
- 10-year: 4.1% (stable, not breaking lower)
- RIKB auction data captured was ICELANDIC bonds (search error) — US data still needed
- MOVE index level: NOT YET CAPTURED — critical gap

TLT VERDICT: No immediate catalyst for the ~115bps rally $101C needs. The $90C position
is more realistic. Watch: March FOMC (3/19), Q1 GDP, credit spread widening. Time decay
accelerating — 11 months to 1/15/27 expiry.

### 2. Longevity Research Hit Peak Productivity — 4 Score-5 Entries

The afternoon Reason cycle produced the best research day since tracking began:

a) p-tau217 commercial availability: 8 labs now offering testing (Labcorp test code
   484390, Mayo PT217, Lucent, Genova, TrueHealthLabs, ALZpath, C2N, Quest). Cost
   $195-$400. FDA cleared Lumipulse G pTau217/AB42 ratio May 2025. Insurance coverage
   expanding. ACTIONABLE: Matt should consider testing given 3-4 year prediction window.

b) AKL003 Phase 1b: Launched 2/19/2026 (2 days ago), actively recruiting ages 25-75.
   Alpha Klotho mRNA in LNPs, IV administration, 2:1 randomization, single site at GARM
   Clinic in Prospera, Roatan, Honduras. NOT on ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT07216781 unclear).

c) DMTF1: Science Advances Jan 2026 (DOI: 10.1126/sciadv.ady5905). DMTF1 upregulation
   rescues aged neural
priorities_content:
# Research Priorities — Updated 2026-03-01

## Tier 1 — Actively Monitor
1. **Rapamycin clinical trials** — PEARL trial Phase 2 ongoing; watch for full
   results. Human safety/efficacy data is the gap to fill. Source: pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov
2. **AI agent reliability** — Tool-calling accuracy is the limiting factor for
   Zeke autonomy. Monitor Berkeley BFCL leaderboard for model improvements.
   Current ceiling: ~88% multi-step. Gap: real-world reliability below benchmark.

## Tier 2 — Periodic Check (Weekly)
3. **Spaced repetition implementation** — Apply double-spacing intervals to Zeke
   feed review. Research is settled; priority shifts to implementation.
4. **Treasury yields** — 10Y at 3.95%, Fed holding. Monitor for rate cut signals
   that affect TLT position.

## Tier 3 — Diminishing Returns (Reduce Feed Weight)
5. **tool-calling general** — Saturated. Consolidate to monthly summary.
6. **compound-synthesis** — Derivable locally; not worth dedicated feed slots.
7. **AI agent benchmarks (general)** — High noise/signal ratio; filter to
   specific named tools only (Claude Code, Lovable, Cursor).

## Feed Health Notes
- Dedup pass needed: tool-calling topic has 8+ near-identical entries this cycle
- compound-synthesis entries should be generated locally, not ingested as feed
- Quality scoring working well; 4-5 scores are surfacing genuinely useful content

strategic_context:
# Zeke Strategic Context
Last distilled: 2026-03-02

## System Architecture

**Compute Stack**: DGX Spark (10.0.0.143:11434) running local models for autonomous inference. Active models: qwen3-32b-32k (reasoning), glm-4.7-flash (analysis), qwen2.5-coder:32b (coding). Main chat routes to Claude Sonnet (quality guarantee). Subagents → local models (cost control: $0 variable cost). Fallback chain: spark → spark → anthropic/claude.

**Memory Layer**: 16 daily OpenClaw logs (2026-02-01 through 2026-02-19), session-journal.jsonl (13+ entries through 2026-03-01), anti-patterns.md (6 operational rules learned). MEMORY.md canonical (200-line constraint). Feed: 649 entries across 6 canonical domains (treasury-bonds, camel-finance, ai-agents, longevity, compound-synthesis, operation). Memory search via nomic-embed-text embeddings. Nightly distillation: reads OpenClaw daily files → haiku synthesis → claude-strategic-context.md refresh.

**Queue Architecture**: spark-work-queue.py daemon continuously processes synthesis task queue. Feed growth: depends on health of 6 domain ingestion pipelines (MacroAlf RSS, TreasuryDirect auctions, Camel Finance YouTube transcripts, Huberman Lab episodes, Bryan Johnson longevity, Attia Research). Current anomaly: feed growth -1937 entries (Feb 5–12 outage recovery not yet complete).

**Approval Layer**: pending-approval.json stores 8 decision types (request_approval, resolve_approval, CLI: python3 zeke-approval.py). Human-in-the-loop wiring live as of Block 7. Approval IDs converted from hex to human-readable slugs (e.g., remediation-camel-finance-001). Quality-weights.py triggers approvals for WEAK domains or declining trends. Session init: pending_approvals summary displayed with approve_does/reject_does legible to human.

**LaunchAgent Daemons** (6 active, all running on local models):
- 4:45 AM: Memory distillation (zeke-memory-distill.py) — 16 daily files + journal → strategic-context
